response.justification: The candidate is a capable full-stack and systems developer with strong testing culture and broad polyglot experience, but critically fails to meet the most weighted minimum qualification: 8 years of C++ experience. Professional C++ exposure is limited to approximately 6 months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project. The role also requires 5 years building large-scale infrastructure or cloud compute platforms; while the candidate has touched distributed systems (Dunnhumby micro-services) and cloud tooling (AWS CDK), there is no evidence of owning or leading a compute platform at the required depth or scale. The overall profile skews toward web application development and developer tooling rather than systems-level cloud infrastructure leadership.
strengths: Demonstrated commitment to engineering craft: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and community knowledge-sharing across multiple roles
Experience operating in high-throughput distributed environments, including micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Dunnhumby
Staff-level seniority with informal mentoring track record via workshops, code katas, and conference talks
concerns: C++ experience is critically insufficient: roughly 6 months professionally versus the 8-year minimum required by the role
No evidence of designing, building, or leading an internal cloud compute platform or equivalent large-scale infrastructure initiative
No formal technical leadership role managing cloud or DevOps engineers; mentoring has been community-oriented rather than organisational
